market_context.diagnostic_focus: wing-forward rebound environment and team glass imbalance

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.summary: Charlotte controlled the glass from start to finish and finished plus-19 on rebounds, creating a strong wing-forward board environment. New York failed to generate normal center-level rebound volume, which pushed more rebound access toward Josh Hart, while Charlotte's length and activity created enough total miss volume for Miles Bridges to clear his small number cleanly.
key_distribution_notes: Charlotte rebound share was spread across Knueppel (11), Brandon Miller (8), LaMelo Ball (5), Miles Bridges (5), Diabate (5), and Kalkbrenner (4).
Karl-Anthony Towns was held to just 3 rebounds in 22 minutes, materially shifting New York's usable rebound share outward.
Josh Hart again functioned as a cleanup rebounder and led the Knicks with 7 boards in 39 minutes.

notes: Charlotte's plus-7 offensive rebounding edge and plus-19 total rebounding edge drove the environment we wanted to attack.
Knueppel's unexpected 11 rebounds amplified Charlotte's team-glass dominance and validated the broad rebound availability on the Hornets side.
Towns' suppressed minutes and rebound total reduced competition for Hart's board path, allowing Hart to get there despite the Knicks losing badly on the glass overall.
Charlotte played with more energy and won the possession battle through second chances and cleaner defensive rebounding.
New York's frontcourt stability was weaker than expected in practice, forcing Hart into a familiar high-activity support role on the glass.
Miles Bridges did not need a spike game; the line was simply low enough for his normal role to clear in a positive team rebounding environment.

hits: Miles Bridges over 4.5 rebounds was correctly priced as a modest but live wing-forward edge.
Josh Hart over 6.5 rebounds remained viable because his role insulates him from center volatility on the Knicks side.
Hornets-vs-Knicks matchup correctly flagged as a favorable rebound environment for secondary rebounders.
patch_recommendations: Keep Hart protected by role-based rebound stability when Towns/Robinson minute or rebound concentration looks fragile.
Continue targeting low wing-forward lines in Charlotte games when team rebound distribution is broad rather than center-locked.
Add a team-glass imbalance trigger when one side projects to win the rebound battle by 8+ and the market prices secondary players too conservatively.

final_verdict.overall: Clean two-leg rebound card win driven by correct environment read and proper role weighting.
final_verdict.confidence_takeaway: Trust wing-forward rebound overs more aggressively when Charlotte's distributed glass profile meets a Knicks setup where Hart can inherit cleanup work from unstable center production.
